eye.description: The practice is licensed in terms of relevant legislation for the level and type of services provided.
eye.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.

eye.description: The manager ensures that there is a system in place to monitor the implementation of applicable laws and regulations.
eye.guideline: This criterion will be scored compliant by default, although a PC rating is given whenever there is definite evidence of non-adherence to any legal requirement. In these cases, the transgression needs to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC rating and it should be based on accurate facts. Common examples of non-conformance with legal requirements include the following deficiencies: The required certificates are not available, for example, fire clearance certificates, electrical installation certificates, commissioning certificates (for example, ethylene oxide sterilisers), pressure test certificates for vessels under pressure Internal/external financial audits are not conducted Nurses transcribe doctors’ prescriptions and/or dispense medication Pharmaceutical items are incorrectly labelled Proof of current registration of professional personnel with the relevant councils is not available In order to demonstrate compliance, practices may wish to compile a template listing the criteria requiring demonstration of compliance with country-specific legislation and regulations against the relevant national acts, regulations, etc. applicable to the criterion.
eye.guidelines: This criterion will be scored compliant by default, although a PC rating is given whenever there is definite evidence of non-adherence to any legal requirement. In these cases, the transgression needs to be recorded in detail to motivate for the PC rating and it should be based on accurate facts. Common examples of non-conformance with legal requirements include the following deficiencies: The required certificates are not available, for example, fire clearance certificates, electrical installation certificates, commissioning certificates (for example, ethylene oxide sterilisers), pressure test certificates for vessels under pressure Internal/external financial audits are not conducted Nurses transcribe doctors’ prescriptions and/or dispense medication Pharmaceutical items are incorrectly labelled Proof of current registration of professional personnel with the relevant councils is not available In order to demonstrate compliance, practices may wish to compile a template listing the criteria requiring demonstration of compliance with country-specific legislation and regulations against the relevant national acts, regulations, etc. applicable to the criterion.

eye.description: There is evidence of response to any reports from inspecting and regulatory authorities
eye.guideline: CRITICAL  This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections that may have been conducted. Examples may include inspections by the following authorities: The local fire authority for fire clearance purposes The national authority responsible for radiation safety aspects The national authority responsible for health and safety compliance The national authority responsible for financial compliance The national authority responsible for maintenance requirements such as boiler inspections and pressure tests, autoclave pressure tests The local authority responsible for testing of water supplies The relevant professional bodies where student training is provided Licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc. Licensing authorities for facilities. Immigration requirements for non-resident
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL  This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections that may have been conducted. Examples may include inspections by the following authorities: The local fire authority for fire clearance purposes The national authority responsible for radiation safety aspects The national authority responsible for health and safety compliance The national authority responsible for financial compliance The national authority responsible for maintenance requirements such as boiler inspections and pressure tests, autoclave pressure tests The local authority responsible for testing of water supplies The relevant professional bodies where student training is provided Licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc. Licensing authorities for facilities. Immigration requirements for non-resident

eye.description: The manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
eye.guideline: The criterion will be scored after the final assessment of Management and Leadership criteria and will be scored according to the findings of financial and human resource management and other criteria dealing with adequate supply and effective management of resources (medication, consumables, equipment etc.). Linked criteria: 1.4.1.1-  1.4.1.3 1.4.2.1-  1.4.2.4 1.4.3.1-  1.4.3.4 1.5.1.1-  1.5.2.4 1.5.2.1- 1.5.2.4 1.5.3.1- 1.5.3.6 1.6.1.1- 1.6.1.4
eye.guidelines: The criterion will be scored after the final assessment of Management and Leadership criteria and will be scored according to the findings of financial and human resource management and other criteria dealing with adequate supply and effective management of resources (medication, consumables, equipment etc.). Linked criteria: 1.4.1.1-  1.4.1.3 1.4.2.1-  1.4.2.4 1.4.3.1-  1.4.3.4 1.5.1.1-  1.5.2.4 1.5.2.1- 1.5.2.4 1.5.3.1- 1.5.3.6 1.6.1.1- 1.6.1.4

eye.description: Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to services in the community who work in collaboration with the practice.
eye.guideline: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by for example, email correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services.
eye.guidelines: This information needs to be provided for example, to community nursing teams, step down care, allied health professionals etc. Compliance can be demonstrated by for example, email correspondence with the various services or by providing information pamphlets about the practice to these services.

eye.description: There is a process to ensure that personnel are familiar with relevant policies and procedures.
There are documented processes for staffing the practice.
eye.guideline: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.
eye.guidelines: Compliance with this criterion will require the practitioner to show documented evidence that patient load and patient acuity were studied and considered when planning the staffing for the practice. This must be evident for both the number of personnel required as well as their qualifications.

eye.description: The desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge are defined for personnel.
eye.guideline: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.
eye.guidelines: These requirements must be discussed and agreed by all relevant personnel during the recruitment process and form part of the position description.

eye.description: The process includes measures to improve personnel retention.
eye.guideline: Retention of personnel as opposed to the recruitment of new personnel is more cost effective and more likely to result in better patient care due to improved continuity of care and service provision and the accumulation of experience. It is therefore strategically important for retention to be considered as part of the recruitment process. As an additional benefit, measures taken to improve retention are likely to improve personnel satisfaction, and satisfied personnel provide better services. For a practice to be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of at least a personnel satisfaction survey.
eye.guidelines: Retention of personnel as opposed to the recruitment of new personnel is more cost effective and more likely to result in better patient care due to improved continuity of care and service provision and the accumulation of experience. It is therefore strategically important for retention to be considered as part of the recruitment process. As an additional benefit, measures taken to improve retention are likely to improve personnel satisfaction, and satisfied personnel provide better services. For a practice to be compliant with this criterion evidence must be provided of at least a personnel satisfaction survey.

eye.description: There is implementation of a reliable, documented process for evaluating and verifying the credentials of all health professionals.
eye.guideline: This can be achieved for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee's personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.
eye.guidelines: This can be achieved for example, by having a standardised checklist of requirements that must be verified. This should include reference checks. The completed document can then be retained in the employee's personnel file. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of professional personnel.

eye.description: Personnel files contain copies of qualifications and licences/registration from the relevant authority for all health professionals.
eye.guideline: CRITICAL  Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL  Copies of all the relevant original degrees/diplomas/certificates must be available, as well as evidence of registration with the relevant registration bodies. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files.

eye.description: There is a system to track the annual registration of all health professionals.
eye.guideline: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional's licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.
eye.guidelines: The system must reflect the date that each category of health professional's licence is due for renewal and document that the original, renewed licence has been seen.

eye.description: All personnel with direct contact with the public have had a police check, a copy of which is kept in their personnel file.
eye.guideline: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.
eye.guidelines: Police checks are repeated every 3 years or as appropriate. Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel file.

eye.description: There are documented processes for orientation of personnel to the practice.
eye.guideline: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.
eye.guidelines: This includes general orientation and position-specific orientation as discussed in the standard intent above.

eye.description: The practice supportscontinuing education for its clinical personnel andmaintains records of this in personnel files.
eye.guideline: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.
eye.guidelines: This refers specifically to professional personnel and the requirements for continued registration with the relevant professional bodies, where applicable. Management must have a clear strategy for assisting professional personnel to maintain their continued registration.

eye.description: There is a development strategy for the practice that ensures that the practicemanager and administrative personnel receive the training required to fulfil their responsibilities.
eye.guideline: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room.
eye.guidelines: This training could include billing, medical aid codes and triaging, (which is particularly important when administrative personnel are responsible for the initial assessment of patients in the waiting room.

eye.description: Personnel members are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research, and other experiences.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during an audit of the personnel files of various categories of personnel or other training records. Personnel members may also be interviewed.

eye.description: A designated person is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of a financial strategy.
eye.guideline: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.
eye.guidelines: This requires that an individual in the practice has the officially assigned duties of overseeing and taking responsibility for all aspects of financial management. This may be the practice manager, a financial manager or a shared responsibility between practice manager and a financial officer.

eye.description: The designated person is suitably qualified and/or experienced in accounting and financial management.
eye.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
eye.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

eye.description: The responsibilities of this person include ensuring that policies and procedures for all financial functions are implemented.
eye.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.
eye.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' finances. Where a financial manager or financial officer is employed, policies and procedures must guide their functions.

eye.description: There is a current budget for the practice.
eye.guideline: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. For practices that comprise of a solo practitioner there should be evidence of a practice budget. The budget must demonstrate allocation of resources. The budget will include, for example, salaries, rent, utility costs (water and electricity), consumable stock etc.
eye.guidelines: For practices that comprise of more than one practitioner there should be an approved budget available in the management documentation. For practices that comprise of a solo practitioner there should be evidence of a practice budget. The budget must demonstrate allocation of resources. The budget will include, for example, salaries, rent, utility costs (water and electricity), consumable stock etc.

eye.description: A report is produced at least annually, setting out the financial position to date.
eye.guideline: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.
eye.guidelines: This criterion requires that at least the most recent financial report that is prepared annually for submission to the relevant authorities such as Botswana Unified Revenue Service (BURS) must be available during the survey.

eye.description: There is a mechanism for establishing the reason for budget variation in either income or expenditure.
eye.guideline: Evidence should exist in the format in which financial statements are produced. Generally, these statements contain a column/section indicating the variance, i.e. under- or over-expenditure. Evidence showing what is done about this variance (especially over-expenditure), should be available, for example, in the minutes of relevant financial management meetings, personnel meetings or memos.
eye.guidelines: Evidence should exist in the format in which financial statements are produced. Generally, these statements contain a column/section indicating the variance, i.e. under- or over-expenditure. Evidence showing what is done about this variance (especially over-expenditure), should be available, for example, in the minutes of relevant financial management meetings, personnel meetings or memos.

eye.description: Capital investment proposals are subject to unanimous agreement among the partners or are agreed according to a voting system acceptable to all partners in the service.
eye.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored "not applicable" in practices owned by a solo practitioner.
eye.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored "not applicable" in practices owned by a solo practitioner.

eye.description: The practicehave documented ethical and legal policies and procedures for the financial management of the practice that are implemented.
eye.guideline: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).
eye.guidelines: Examples of compliance with this criterion can be found in the policies and procedures for billing or referral to partners within the practice (for example partners in a poly clinic).

eye.description: Internal and external financial audit systems which meet audit requirements are maintained.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as Botswana Companies Act.

eye.description: Annual audited financial statements are produced within the required time frame.
eye.guideline: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL Compliance will be measured in accordance with the national requirements such as of the Botswana Companies Act.

eye.description: There is a capital asset register, which is routinely maintained.
eye.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.
eye.guidelines: Documented evidence will be required.

eye.description: Assets are insured.
eye.guideline: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.
eye.guidelines: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.

eye.description: All health professionals provide evidence of professional indemnity insurance.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.

eye.description: An individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the service practice.
eye.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' equipment and supplies. Where a supply chain manager/stock controller is employed, or the function is performed by someone other than the solo practice manager, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
eye.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices' equipment and supplies. Where a supply chain manager/stock controller is employed, or the function is performed by someone other than the solo practice manager, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

eye.description: Policies and procedures relating to all aspects of provisioning/supply chain management are implemented.
eye.guideline: As a minimum, the policies and procedures discussed in a) – d) in the standard intent above should be available, accessible to personnel and implementation should be monitored.
eye.guidelines: As a minimum, the policies and procedures discussed in a) – d) in the standard intent above should be available, accessible to personnel and implementation should be monitored.

eye.description: Secure storage facilities are available.
eye.guideline: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.
eye.guidelines: The store must be lockable and exclude any unauthorised entry.

eye.description: Prescription pads, letterheads, investigation request forms, administrative records and other official documents are accessible only to authorised persons.
Regular in-service training is given to all personnel in the field of infection control and is documented.
eye.guideline: The infection control education programme needs to include policies/guidelines as well as relevant issues as they are identified. All personnel should be included in information sharing and training at regular scheduled meetings or other fora. Documented evidence (for example, minutes of meetings/attendance records) must be provided.
eye.guidelines: The infection control education programme needs to include policies/guidelines as well as relevant issues as they are identified. All personnel should be included in information sharing and training at regular scheduled meetings or other fora. Documented evidence (for example, minutes of meetings/attendance records) must be provided.

eye.description: The practicereports on notifiable diseases to appropriate external public health agencies.
eye.guideline: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.

eye.description: The practice has a waste management policy that is consistent with country-specific laws and regulations and includes the safe handling, storing and disposing of all different types of waste.
eye.guideline: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.
eye.guidelines: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.

eye.description: Security systems, including guards if required, provide for internal and external security.
eye.guideline: If the security system is outsourced (alarms/guards) the service contract must be available during the survey. As a minimum, a practice must be secured with burglar bars and security gates. Compliance will be further measured by observation of the security measures in place and whether they are appropriate for the individual setting.
eye.guidelines: If the security system is outsourced (alarms/guards) the service contract must be available during the survey. As a minimum, a practice must be secured with burglar bars and security gates. Compliance will be further measured by observation of the security measures in place and whether they are appropriate for the individual setting.

eye.description: There is effective control of access to clinical areas and store areas.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation.

eye.description: Reasonable measures are taken to ensure the safety of lone workers.
eye.guideline: This criterion is only applicable where practitioners consult with patients after hours. Reasonable measures can include for example, the installation of panic buttons or notifying another person or security company that the practitioner is alone in the practice. Sufficient arrangements should be in place to ensure that a personnel member who is alone in the practice is safe. These arrangements must be outlined in a policy which all personnel should be aware of.
eye.guidelines: This criterion is only applicable where practitioners consult with patients after hours. Reasonable measures can include for example, the installation of panic buttons or notifying another person or security company that the practitioner is alone in the practice. Sufficient arrangements should be in place to ensure that a personnel member who is alone in the practice is safe. These arrangements must be outlined in a policy which all personnel should be aware of.

eye.description: There are structured systems and processes in place to ensure that all occupants of the practice's facilities are safe from fire or smoke.
eye.guideline: There are documented fire safety systems which include all the relevant aspects of fire safety, for example training, rehearsals, servicing and storage of equipment, escape route signage, storage and handling of flammable materials, etc.
eye.guidelines: There are documented fire safety systems which include all the relevant aspects of fire safety, for example training, rehearsals, servicing and storage of equipment, escape route signage, storage and handling of flammable materials, etc.

eye.description: Documented certification is available from the relevant authority to show that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety (Fire clearance certificate).
eye.guideline: CRITICAL As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. The practice manager must obtain a copy of the documentation from the landlord if the practice does not own the building.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL As per the intent statement, compliance with this criterion requires documented evidence of an official inspection to confirm that the building complies with national fire safety requirements. The practice manager must obtain a copy of the documentation from the landlord if the practice does not own the building.

eye.description: Documented evidence is available to show that the personnel participate in a rehearsal of the emergency plan at least annually.
eye.guideline: The emphasis of this rehearsal must be on the emergency situations mentioned in the standard intent. Documented evidence must be provided.
eye.guidelines: The emphasis of this rehearsal must be on the emergency situations mentioned in the standard intent. Documented evidence must be provided.

eye.description: Documented procedures which outline the processes to provide required information to individuals and agencies outside the practicewhen required by laws or regulations are implemented.
eye.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.
eye.guidelines: CRITICAL  Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.

eye.description: Personnel are trained in the implementation of quality management processes.
eye.guideline: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.
eye.guidelines: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.

eye.description: Directional signs to the practice are clearly readable and up to date.
eye.guideline: This requirement ensures that members of the public, both locals and visitors, who are not familiar with the location of the practice will be able to find it when necessary.
eye.guidelines: This requirement ensures that members of the public, both locals and visitors, who are not familiar with the location of the practice will be able to find it when necessary.

eye.description: A telephone/emergency number is available and provided to patients on registration and on request.
eye.guideline: This can be in the form of a business card or patient information leaflet. Where the practice does not provide emergency services the emergency number must reflect the closest emergency facility to the practice.
eye.guidelines: This can be in the form of a business card or patient information leaflet. Where the practice does not provide emergency services the emergency number must reflect the closest emergency facility to the practice.

eye.description: A phone number for the after-hours service of the practice is clearly displayed on the outside of the facility.
eye.guideline: Where the practice does not offer an after-hours service, an emergency number must be displayed. Compliance will be verified by observation.
eye.guidelines: Where the practice does not offer an after-hours service, an emergency number must be displayed. Compliance will be verified by observation.

eye.description: The supply of medical equipment is adequate to meet the needs of the practice.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation and during personnel and patient interviews.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be verified by observation and during personnel and patient interviews.

eye.description: Adequate, secure and well-ventilated storage areas are available for cleaning and laundry equipment and chemicals.
eye.guideline: Chemicals for cleaning and laundry are safely stored in a well-ventilated, locked cupboard or room out of the reach of patients, children and visitors. There is adequate storage place for brooms and mops. Compliance will be measured by observation.
eye.guidelines: Chemicals for cleaning and laundry are safely stored in a well-ventilated, locked cupboard or room out of the reach of patients, children and visitors. There is adequate storage place for brooms and mops. Compliance will be measured by observation.

eye.description: The credentials of alternative care providers are verified.
Medication is prescribed and dispensed in accordance with legislation and current pharmaceutical, medical and eye guidelines.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

eye.description: Medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by policy.
A register is maintained of all medication dispensed.
eye.guideline: Documented evidence is required.
eye.guidelines: Documented evidence is required.

eye.description: Controlled medication is prescribed and dispensed in accordance with legislation and practice policy.
Prescriptions conform to all legal requirements.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

eye.description: Policies and procedures are implemented to ensure that medication is transported to the practiceand stored within the practice according to manufacturers’ guidelines, with specific emphasis on maintenance of cold chain requirements.
eye.guideline: Guideline statement:  Policies must be available that address the transportation of medication and particularly the maintenance of the cold chain all the way from the manufacturer to the end user. It is the responsibility of the dispensing practitioner to ensure that the cold chain is maintained. Documented evidence of cold-chain monitoring will be required for compliance with this criterion.
eye.guidelines: Guideline statement:  Policies must be available that address the transportation of medication and particularly the maintenance of the cold chain all the way from the manufacturer to the end user. It is the responsibility of the dispensing practitioner to ensure that the cold chain is maintained. Documented evidence of cold-chain monitoring will be required for compliance with this criterion.

eye.description: Medication storage areas are protected from heat and light and effectively ventilated.
eye.guideline: Note must be made of provision for medication that should not be exposed to direct sunlight. Where the ambient temperature in the cupboard/room cannot be maintained at 20-25 degrees Celsius there should be evidence that the environmental temperature is monitored and complies with the manufacturer’s requirements for medication storage.
eye.guidelines: Note must be made of provision for medication that should not be exposed to direct sunlight. Where the ambient temperature in the cupboard/room cannot be maintained at 20-25 degrees Celsius there should be evidence that the environmental temperature is monitored and complies with the manufacturer’s requirements for medication storage.

eye.description: Stored medication is legibly marked and securely labelled.
eye.guideline: Country-specific regulations apply, but must include at least the following: Proprietary name/approved name or name of each active ingredient Strength and number of dose units in the container Expiry date and batch number
eye.guidelines: Country-specific regulations apply, but must include at least the following: Proprietary name/approved name or name of each active ingredient Strength and number of dose units in the container Expiry date and batch number

eye.description: All pharmaceuticals and medical consumables are regularly checked for expiry dates and checks are recorded.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be measured by observation and documented evidence is required.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be measured by observation and documented evidence is required.

eye.description: Patient and family rights are identified in a policy and documented in accordance with relevant and current laws, charters and regulations.
eye.guideline: Patient and family rights are internationally accepted and implemented. Country specific patient rights charters must be referenced in the policy. The practice policy must include at least the right to: A healthy and safe environment Participation in decision-making Access to health care Ethical health care Choice of health services Confidentiality and privacy Culturally sensitive care Informed consent Refusal of treatment A second opinion Complaints about health services
eye.guidelines: Patient and family rights are internationally accepted and implemented. Country specific patient rights charters must be referenced in the policy. The practice policy must include at least the right to: A healthy and safe environment Participation in decision-making Access to health care Ethical health care Choice of health services Confidentiality and privacy Culturally sensitive care Informed consent Refusal of treatment A second opinion Complaints about health services

eye.description: The practice has a documented policy outlining the procedure for obtaining consent in the case of patients who are unable to grant consent for themselves by way of age or mental/physical incapacity, which is in accordance with the relevant laws and regulations.
eye.guideline: Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.
eye.guidelines: Compliance will be assessed during the patient record audit.
